summary:
Passive diffusion is the predominant mode of drug transport across biological membranes due to its efficiency and independence from cellular energy requirements. Other methods such as facilitated diffusion and active transport play secondary roles compared to passive diffusion. To summarize:

Passive diffusion predominates in transporting drugs across cell membranes because it does not require energy; instead, it relies on natural concentration gradients. Facilitated diffusion and active transport serve additional functions but are less common. Summary ends here. 

The summary has been condensed into two coherent statements focusing solely on critical aspects related directly addressing the core query about major transportation mechanisms without extraneous details.
